title: plagiobothrys collinus var. californicus
intro: Plagiobothrys collinus var. californicus, also known as California wild heliotrope or California pricklepoppy, is a perennial plant in the Boraginaceae family, commonly found in the western United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its saponin and alkaloid content. The health benefits of Plagiobothrys collinus var. californicus include soothing skin irritations, treating wounds, and reducing fever, as well as supporting respiratory health and relieving coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
